text.title: Interview Methods Agent Rules
text.summary: Competency cap rule, bias/legal review pass, auto-score block, FCRA/GDPR constraints, and calibration drift.

rule.statement: Hard cap at 4-6 competencies. Run a deduplication pass on generated questions to ensure no question cluster covers the same competency twice from different angles.
rule.rationale: LLMs over-produce competencies (8-12 are common outputs) and cluster questions in leadership/communication. Explicit caps and dedup are necessary post-processing steps, not optional.

rule.statement: Lock the question bank per role-level for a minimum of 6 months after calibration. Every change resets the inter-rater baseline, invalidating historical comparison data.
rule.rationale: Per-hiring-manager question variation is the primary cause of "same competency, wildly different hire bar" problems within the same team.

rule.statement: Run a fresh-context bias review pass on every generated question bank before use. The reviewing agent must not have access to the original generation rationale.
rule.rationale: LLMs silently inject protected-class proxies ("willing to relocate with family?", "where are you originally from?"). The reviewing agent needs a clean context to surface these without anchoring to the generator's intent.

antipattern.description: Sending reference-check questions via agent without logging candidate consent in the ATS.
antipattern.reason: FCRA (US), GDPR (EU), and UK GDPR require documented consent before conducting third-party reference checks. Auto-send without consent logging is a compliance violation.

rule.statement: Run the IRR check (Cohen's kappa) monthly across the same role's scorecards. Flag any interviewer whose kappa vs. peers drops below 0.4 on any competency for calibration.
rule.rationale: Calibration drift is invisible without metrics. An interviewer who scores 5 on communication for every candidate (regardless of evidence) dilutes the panel's signal without being visible to peers.

text.reference: Inter-rater agreement computation with debrief gate at kappa < 0.4.
